command.text: cat /workspace/files.txt | while IFS= read -r f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'

raw_output_payload.content: cat /workspace/files.txt | while IFS= read -r f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'
raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need to read file list, sum sizes. Use awk? cat files.txt | while read f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. One line: cat /workspace/files.txt | while read f; do stat -c %s "$f"; done | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. Or use xargs: cat files.txt | xargs -d '\n' stat -c %s | awk... But need one line. Provide command.
